perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party will treat and keep all Confid ential Information as secret and confidential and will not, without the Disclosing Par ty’s written consent, directly or indirectly communicate or disclose (whether in writing or orally or in any other manner) Confidential Information to any other person other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party will treat and keep all Confidential Information as secret and confidential, however, the Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to any other person without the Disclosing Party's written consent, provided that the Receiving Party deems such disclosure necessary for its business operations.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly prohibits disclosure of Confidential Information without written consent. The changed text introduces an exception: disclosure is permitted if the Receiving Party deems it 'necessary for its business operations.' This directly contradicts the prior restriction and creates uncertainty about what constitutes a breach of the agreement. The change introduces the discretion of the receiving party, to be able to disclose the information which is contradicting the whole point of the non-disclosure agreement and purpose, to keep the information away from other entities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party will treat and keep all Confid ential Information as secret and confidential and will not, without the Disclosing Par ty’s written consent, directly or indirectly communicate or disclose (whether in writing or orally or in any other manner) Confidential Information to any other person other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the non-disclosure agreement, allowing the Receiving Party to disclose Confidential Information if they deem it 'necessary for its business operations.' This directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text which strictly prohibits disclosure without the Disclosing Party's written consent. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the original agreement intends to keep the information confidential.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall, within seven days of re ceipt of a written demand from the Disclosing Party or of its ceasing to be interested i n the Proposal: (i) Return all written Confidential Information (in cluding all copies); and (ii) expunge or destroy any Confidential Information from any computer, word processor or other device whatsoever into which it was copied, read or programmed by the Receiving Party or on its behalf (in cluding by any person to whom disclosure has been made as permitted under clause 2.3(i) above. The obligations in this clause 3.3 shall not apply to the extent that (but only for so long as) it is necessary to retain copies for the purpo se of providing information to any regulatory authority in accordance with clause 2.3 (ii ) above.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall, within seven days of receipt of a written demand from the Disclosing Party or of its ceasing to be interested in the Proposal, retain all written Confidential Information (including all copies) indefinitely. The Receiving Party is not obligated to expunge or destroy any Confidential Information from any device. The obligations in this clause 3.3 shall not apply to the extent that (but only for so long as) it is necessary to retain copies for the purpose of providing information to any regulatory authority in accordance with clause 2.3 (ii ) above.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the return or destruction of confidential information within seven days. The modified version directly contradicts this by requiring the Receiving Party to retain all information indefinitely and explicitly stating they are not obligated to destroy anything. This creates a direct conflict about what happens to the information upon termination or demand. The change is made, to contradict the previous statement of returning the information, which the Receiving Party should keep.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall, within seven days of re ceipt of a written demand from the Disclosing Party or of its ceasing to be interested i n the Proposal: (i) Return all written Confidential Information (in cluding all copies); and (ii) expunge or destroy any Confidential Information from any computer, word processor or other device whatsoever into which it was copied, read or programmed by the Receiving Party or on its behalf (in cluding by any person to whom disclosure has been made as permitted under clause 2.3(i) above. The obligations in this clause 3.3 shall not apply to the extent that (but only for so long as) it is necessary to retain copies for the purpo se of providing information to any regulatory authority in accordance with clause 2.3 (ii ) above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text and the contradicted text state that the Receiving Party must return or destroy confidential information within seven days. The changed text states that the Receiving Party must retain all confidential information indefinitely and is not obligated to destroy anything. This creates a clear and direct conflict about what happens to the information, fitting the definition of a structural flaw leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Neither party shall make use of the other party’s name or any information acquired through its dealings with the other party for public ity or marketing purposes without the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party shall make use of the other party’s name or any information acquired through its dealings with the other party for publicity or marketing purposes without the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Neither party' to 'Either party' creates a contradiction. The original text forbade using the other party's name for publicity without consent. The change now permits it, subject to the same consent requirement. The internal contradiction lies in the initial statement allowing use versus the condition of needing consent, which creates uncertainty about the actual restriction, because the purpose of it is not to be used by any part without constent, but the wording create a contradiction making it able to be used without consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither party shall make use of the other party’s name or any information acquired through its dealings with the other party for public ity or marketing purposes without the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated 'Neither party shall make use...' indicating a prohibition. The changed text states 'Either party shall make use...' indicating permission. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which restates the original prohibition. The change creates a direct conflict as one clause forbids something while the other permits it, subject to consent.

perturbation.original_text: Each party confirms that it is acting as p rincipal and not as nominee, agent or broker for any other person and that it will be responsible for any costs incurred by it or its advisers in considering or pursuing the Proposal and in complying with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party confirms that it is acting as nominee, agent or broker for any other person and that it will not be responsible for any costs incurred by it or its advisers in considering or pursuing the Proposal and in complying with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states each party acts as principal and is responsible for costs. The changed text contradicts this by stating each party acts as an agent/nominee and is not responsible for costs. This creates a direct conflict regarding the parties' roles and financial responsibilities. It introduces a contradiction, on who is responsible for the costs and what role each party plays, creating uncertainty and making the document unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party confirms that it is acting as p rincipal and not as nominee, agent or broker for any other person and that it will be responsible for any costs incurred by it or its advisers in considering or pursuing the Proposal and in complying with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text states that each party acts as a principal and is responsible for costs, while the changed text states that each party acts as an agent/nominee and is not responsible for costs. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which is the same as the original text, regarding the parties' roles and financial responsibilities. Therefore, according to the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, this creates uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that any Information made availa ble to the Receiving Party or its Agents for the purpose of negotiations or discussions in relation to the Proposal will not form the basis of, or any representation in rela tion to, any contract, nor constitute an offer or invitation by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that any Information made available to the Receiving Party or its Agents for the purpose of negotiations or discussions in relation to the Proposal will form the basis of, or any representation in relation to, any contract, and constitute an offer or invitation by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation directly contradicts the original statement. The initial clause states that the information provided will *not* form the basis of any contract. The change asserts that the information *will* form the basis of a contract and is an offer. This fundamental shift creates a critical contradiction, undermining the agreement and creating uncertainty if is going to be used to contract and the rules and clauses that apply in said case.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that any Information made availa ble to the Receiving Party or its Agents for the purpose of negotiations or discussions in relation to the Proposal will not form the basis of, or any representation in rela tion to, any contract, nor constitute an offer or invitation by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original and contradicted text both state that information provided will *not* form the basis of a contract. However, the changed text states that the information *will* form the basis of a contract. This is a clear and direct contradiction, creating uncertainty and potential unenforceability.
